mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, triggering inflation expectations that force the Fed to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE sustains below 4.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances (RSAFS) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive debt-fueled consumption facing higher financing costs, leading to a sharp earnings downgrade and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive SPX returns occur in >50% of periods where RSAFS and FEDFUNDS both rise simultaneously over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained increase in the industrial production index (INDPRO) reflects capacity utilization rebounding, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises >1.5% quarter-over-quarter during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, it signals a rapid cooling in the housing sector, which historically leads to broader economic contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a >10% monthly drop in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows while real consumer spending (RSAFS) stagnates indicates labor-driven inflation without corresponding demand, eroding profit margins and depressing equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds RSAFS growth by >2 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly correlates with corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings momentum translates to price appreciation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to discount long-duration equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS sustains above 4.5% for two consecutive months.
